flashcards.back: LCM stands for Least Common Multiple. It is the smallest number that is a multiple of two or more given numbers.

Method 1: List multiples of each number and find the smallest common one.

Method 2: Prime factorization - take highest power of each prime factor.
HCF stands for Highest Common Factor (also called GCD - Greatest Common Divisor). It is the largest number that divides two or more numbers evenly.

Factors of 12: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12
Factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18

Common factors: 1, 2, 3, 6
HCF = 6

flashcards.front: How to add fractions with different denominators?
flashcards.back:
Step 1: Find the LCM of the denominators
Step 2: Convert each fraction to equivalent fractions with LCM as denominator
Step 3: Add the numerators
Step 4: Simplify if needed
Motion is the change in position of an object with respect to time and a reference point.
Speed is the rate of change of distance with time. It is a scalar quantity (has only magnitude).
Velocity is the rate of change of displacement with time. It is a vector quantity (has both magnitude and direction).

Speed: Scalar, always positive, uses distance
Velocity: Vector, can be negative, uses displacement

Speed = |Velocity| only for straight-line motion in one direction
Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with time. It is a vector quantity.
Relates final velocity to initial velocity, acceleration, and time.
Relates displacement to initial velocity, acceleration, and time.
Relates final velocity to initial velocity, acceleration, and displacement.
